title: Nigritella runei
intro: Nigritella runei, also known as the purple Nigritella or Nigritella, is a species of orchid native to Europe and the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and digestive issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Nigritella runei include iridoid glycosides, particularly nigritellin, as well as other compounds like alkaloids and phenylethylamine. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Nigritella runei is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Nigritella, which is a group of small to medium-sized orchids. The genus Nigritella is part of the subfamily Orchidinae, which is a subfamily of orchids within the family Orchidaceae. Nigritella runei is native to the high mountains of the Alps, specifically in Italy and Austria. The classification of Nigritella runei is further detailed in the botanical nomenclature system, where it is classified as Nigritella runei (Benedict) Teppner.
